item.title: Buttigieg targeted by fake report to child protective services
item.description: Michigan State Police said law enforcement and Child Protective Services confirmed a report against Pete Buttigieg was unsubstantiated and false.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: Utah declares emergency, limits fireworks as crews battle largest U.S. wildfire
item.description: Utah is restricting fireworks as the largest wildfire in the nation grows, fueled by dry conditions and gusting winds.

item.title: Exonerated man's widow calls settlement in yogurt shop murders "blood money"
item.description: The city of Austin agreed to a multimillion-dollar settlement to be split among four men — including to the widow and daughter of Maurice Pierce — who were wrongfully accused of murdering four teenage girls in a Texas yogurt shop.

item.title: U.S. strikes Iran after Trump accuses Tehran of "foolish violation" of ceasefire
item.description: The U.S. carried out retaliatory strikes against Iran on Friday after Iranian forces hit a cargo vessel in the Strait of Hormuz a day earlier.

item.title: U.S. strikes targets in Iran after drone attack on cargo ship
item.description: The U.S. military says it hit Iranian targets over Iran's drone attack on a commercial vessel in the Strait of Hormuz, marking the first American strikes on Iran since the two countries formally agreed to extend a ceasefire last week.

item.title: Race to find survivors as Venezuela's earthquake death toll soars
item.description: The twin earthquakes that hit Venezuela killed more than 900 people, and that toll is likely to keep rising as frantic rescue and recovery operations ramp up.

item.title: Nurse who went above and beyond gets special send-off: "You're an angel"
item.description: Wynola Wayne received a special retirement send-off after 58 years as a nurse. One former patient, Marco Houpe, said, "If it wasn't for her then, I wouldn't be here today."

item.title: Nurse who went above and beyond gets heartfelt send-off: "You're an angel"
item.description: Steve Hartman goes "On the Road" with the story of a beloved nurse who got no ordinary retirement send-off after 58 years on the job.

item.title: Video shows Boeing 777 making very low pass over Texas airfield
item.description: Data from FlightRadar24 showed the plane was no more than 25 feet above the ground during the low pass as it approached the Horseshoe Bay Resort Jet Center airport.

item.title: Runaway giraffe Gracie found roaming Texas Hill Country after 2-week search
item.description: After spending two weeks on the loose, a giraffe named Gracie was spotted Friday just miles from the Texas ranch she escaped in the Texas Hill Country. Mark Strassmann has more.

item.title: U.S. loses to Turkey in 2026 World Cup, moves to knockout round
item.description: The U.S. Men's National Soccer Team suffered its first loss of the 2026 World Cup Thursday night, but will still advance to the knockout round to face Bosnia and Herzegovina on Wednesday. Nicole Valdes has more.

item.title: U.S. strikes Iran after Trump says Tehran committed "foolish violation" of ceasefire
item.description: The U.S. struck back at Iran Friday after an Iranian drone attack on a cargo ship Thursday in the Strait of Hormuz. CENTCOM said U.S. aircraft hit Iran's missile and drone storage locations. Nikole Killion reports.

item.title: Red flag wildfire warnings issued across 8 Western states
item.description: Red flag wildfire warnings were posted Friday in eight states from the Pacific Northwest to the Desert Southwest, with gusts of dry air creating blowtorch-like conditions. Carter Evans reports.

item.title: FAA investigating low flyover of Boeing 777 at Texas airport
item.description: The Federal Aviation Administration is investigating a low flyover of a Boeing 777 at the Horseshoe Bay Resort Jet Center airport in Texas after a video of the incident went viral. Jason Allen reports.

item.title: Venezuela earthquake death toll tops 900 as rescuers race to find survivors
item.description: The death toll of the Venezuelan earthquakes soared past 900 on Friday. But there is still time - one more day - in what's considered to be the "golden" window for finding survivors. Camilo Montoya-Galvez has the latest.

item.title: What's needed on the ground in Venezuela after the devastating earthquakes
item.description: Rescue teams in Venezuela are racing to dig through the rubble to locate thousands of people who are still missing after two earthquakes rattled the country. Fabio Biolchini, the head of operations for Latin America at Doctors Without Borders, joins "The Daily Report" with more on the immediate needs on the ground.

item.title: JD Vance says, "Violence will be met with violence" after Iran strikes
item.description: The U.S. launched a new round of air strikes one day after an Iranian attack on a cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz. U.S. Central Command said the strikes targeted missile and drone storage locations as well as coastal radar sites. CBS News national security contributor Sam Vinograd has more.

item.title: What we learned from the U.S. loss to Turkey in World Cup ahead of knockout round
item.description: The U.S. Men's National Team is not looking back as it moves into the knockout round in the FIFA Men's World Cup. CBS News' Nicole Valdes has more.

item.title: Early details about U.S. retaliatory strikes on Iran
item.description: The U.S. has conducted new airstrikes on Iran after the country attacked a ship in the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday, the U.S. military says. CBS News national security analyst Aaron MacLean has more details.

item.title: Appeals court upholds Harvey Weinstein's California rape conviction
item.description: A California appeals court has upheld Harvey Weinstein's 2022 rape and sexual assault conviction.

item.title: 6/26: CBS Evening News
item.description: Desperate searches are underway for earthquake survivors in Venezuela; a large jet makes a frighteningly low flyover.

item.title: Emergency crews in Venezuela race to find survivors after earthquakes
item.description: In Venezuela, the confirmed death toll is rapidly growing as rescue teams race to find survivors trapped in the rubble after two devastating earthquakes. Officials report at least 920 are dead in the aftermath, and over 3,300 are injured. CBS News correspondent Cristian Benavides reports.

item.title: U.S. targets missile, drone storage locations in Iran, CENTCOM says
item.description: The U.S. fired retaliatory strikes against Iran on Friday. CENTCOM said the strikes targeted missile and drone storage locations as well as coastal radar sites. The launch is in direct response to an Iranian attack on a Singapore-flagged cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday. CBS News' Kathryn Watson reports.

item.title: 6/26: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: Leon Black testifies before a House panel on the Epstein files; U.S. conducts retaliatory strikes against Iran.

item.title: Minnesota fraud suspect arrested in Somalia after 4 years on the run, officials say
item.description: Abdikerm Eidleh, accused of playing a key role in the Feeding Our Future fraud scheme, was arrested in Somalia after more than four years, federal officials said.

item.title: Venezuela quake survivors describe devastation: "Everything collapsed"
item.description: A Caracas resident told CBS News that he "started to pray" when he felt the first earthquake hit Venezuela.

item.title: Judge in Charlie Kirk murder case finds prosecutor in contempt
item.description: The defense team for Tyler Robinson asked that the death penalty be taken off the table following public comments by prosecutors.

item.title: "Regime Change" authors on Trump's "information bubble" and use of power
item.description: In "Regime Change, Inside the Imperial Presidency of Donald Trump," Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan describe his fundamentally different approach to running the country.

item.title: Newsom proposes federal billionaire tax and AI "public equity" fund
item.description: As California Gov. Gavin Newsom eyes a 2028 presidential bid, he's calling for a national tax on billionaires and a public stake in AI, though he opposes a state ballot measure to tax billionaires.
